prevalence.value: 15-30% (older adults; higher in women and institutionalized)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical assessment with bladder diary, urinalysis, post-void residual when indicated; classify stress/urge/mixed/overflow; exclude UTI and medications

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: low; often not reported unless asked
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improved with behavioral therapy and pelvic floor rehab

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS can diagnose; urology referral for complicated cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle and bladder training, pelvic floor exercises, manage constipation and meds; antimuscarinics/beta-3 agonists when available; treat BPH/overflow contributors
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to pelvic floor physiotherapy and stigma limiting reporting
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Behavioral interventions can be delivered in primary care
